capability_objective: I’m planning for 2026-09-27. Check whether Train fare is available at Chicago and whether Team lunch is available at Boston. Start both at once; on a single transient failure, repeat only that item’s call with identical arguments. Make no persistent changes. Return exactly two bullets and do not include internal error codes.
prompt:
I’m planning for 2026-09-27. Check whether the exact expense item `Train fare` is available at `Chicago` and whether the exact expense item `Team lunch` is available at `Boston`. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `EXPENSES.md`, then use Pi’s genuine Bash tool to run the local `./expense-availability` executable for every availability-data operation. Begin with its built-in `--help` to learn the real interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to expense availability. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database or seed, generated runtime state, signed execution evidence, signing key, setup file, verifier, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not create a script, wrapper, function, loop, or compound shell command for the availability workflow.

In the first assistant action containing availability-data operations, start both fully specified checks as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, with exactly one direct availability operation in each call. Do not combine the checks in one Bash call, use shell backgrounding, or perform either requested check before that paired action. Wait for both calls to finish before taking another action.

If exactly one initial check succeeds and the other reports an explicitly retryable transient failure, retain the successful response. In a later assistant action, repeat only the failed availability check exactly once, using the same executable and identical item, location, and date inputs. Do not repeat the successful check, retry a non-retryable error, change or reorder an input, or use another command or source to reconstruct a result. If both initial checks succeed, do not retry either one. If both fail, a failure is not explicitly retryable, or the one permitted retry fails, stop all availability-data operations and report only the grounded partial outcome. Make no availability-data call after the permitted retry.

This task is read-only. Make no persistent expense-data changes and do not search, create, update, cancel, delete, or notify. Leave every expense and availability row unchanged. Apart from the requested deliverable and runtime state and signed evidence written automatically by the genuine executable, do not create, modify, or delete any file. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete runtime state or execution evidence. Leave no scratch artifacts.

When both checks have successful grounded results, create `availability-report.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines in the requested order, with no heading, blank line, code fence, note, retry discussion, internal error code, raw error text, inventory figure, or extra prose. Format each line exactly as `- <item> at <location>: <availability> on <date>.` Copy item, location, and date from that check’s successful executable response. Render the returned Boolean availability as exactly `available` or `unavailable`. Include no literal angle brackets. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return exactly the same two bullets as the final response and nothing else.
